mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate signals underlying economic weakness and reduced consumer spending power, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and leads to lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where UNRATE is above 8.0%, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales (RSAFS) show a significant acceleration in growth, it indicates strengthening consumer confidence and spending, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS has shown recent growth but below pre-crisis levels
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.5%) in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) and a rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs and reduces future earnings valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS conditions are met, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rebound in housing starts (HOUST) following a period of economic contraction signals a broader recovery in consumer and business confidence, driving increased investment and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST is currently low but showing signs of stabilization post-crisis
failure_condition: If HOUST shows a clear upward trend in a post-recession period, but the S&P 500's 60-day forward returns are predominantly negative (more than 55% of observations), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity and efficient supply chains, which supports corporate profitability and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO has been recovering since the 2008 crisis
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward returns of the S&P 500 show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a deteriorating labor market, signaling an impending economic slowdown or recession, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change is negative for two consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS has been growing, but revisions can be significant
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods following the trigger condition, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate reduces the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, stimulating investment and consumption, thereby supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is currently at a historically low level of 0.07%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive performance during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5% for at least 6 months, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of stagnant or declining real GDP (GDPC1) and elevated consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a stagflationary environment, which erodes corporate profit margins and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth < 1% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Post-crisis GDP growth has been moderate, while inflation is elevated
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of periods where both GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL conditions indicate stagflation, this hypothesis is rejected.
